basics.name: Guimei Liu (刘桂梅)
basics.summary: M.S. candidate in Astrophysics at Shanghai Astronomical Observatory, UCAS. Research interests: observational astrophysics, machine learning, galaxy & open cluster formation and evolution.

work.summary: Identification and characterization of multi-cluster systems in the Milky Way. Advisors: Prof. Li Chen, Prof. Jing Zhong.
highlights: Membership identification using Gaia DR2/EDR3 data with HDBSCAN, pyUPMASK
Distance correction, kinematics and orbit analysis, derivation of physical properties
Master's thesis leading to first-author paper (in preparation)

work.company: Shanghai Astronomy Museum (Branch of Shanghai Science and Technology Museum)
work.summary: Collaborated with Xingmei Shan on summarizing recent progress in the study of open cluster groups in the Milky Way.
highlights: Contributed to second-author paper accepted by Progress in Astronomy

education.institution: University of Chinese Academy of Sciences

education.area: Physics, School of Physics and Space Science

publications.name: New Open-cluster Candidates Found in the Galactic Disk Using Gaia DR2/EDR3 Data
publications.summary: He, Z., Li, C., Zhong, J., Liu, G. et al.

publications.name: Recent Advances in the Study of Binary Star Clusters and Cluster Groups in the Milky Way
publications.summary: SHAN Xing-mei, LIU Gui-mei, ZHONG Jing et al.

publications.name: Multi-cluster systems in the Milky Way (first-author, in preparation)
publications.summary: Liu, G. et al. Master's thesis work.

presentations.name: Probing a complex of triple cluster groups
A hands-on project about instructing high school students in practice of astronomy science research
presentations.event: 4th Shaw-IAU Workshop on Astronomy for Education
